codeReview.summary: The navbar migration is largely aligned with Tamagui structure and preserves wallet integration, but it does not fully satisfy the feature’s responsive breakpoint requirement and has a hover-style mismatch on the search bar.

issues.description: Mobile/collapse behavior is keyed off `media.md` (<=640px), while the feature requires hamburger/collapse behavior at the xl breakpoint. This leaves 641-1024px in desktop layout instead of collapsed behavior (see requirement in mission features.json line 380).

Search bar hover style changes background to `$surface2Hovered` instead of applying a `surface3` border while keeping default border transparent. Feature spec explicitly asks for `border-transparent` default and `hover:surface3` border behavior.

Component introduces hardcoded color literals (`#131313`, `rgba(...)`) instead of theme tokens in several spots (e.g., lines 213, 256, 258, 278-279), which conflicts with mission token-only conventions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Tamagui worker skill mandates TDD test-first for component work, but the web package currently lacks a real component test harness; workers are repeatedly forced to deviate.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill file requires '2. Write Tests First (TDD)' (.factory/skills/tamagui-frontend-worker/SKILL.md:32-33), while handoff reports deviation due to web test script being echo-only (handoff: lines 68-70).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Responsive implementation guidance should document that inline `$gtMd/$gtLg` media props on this `Flex` setup are not type-safe in JSX; workers should prefer `useMedia` or styled-component media definitions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff deviation notes inline `$gtMd/$gtLg` caused TypeScript errors and worker switched to `useMedia` (handoff: lines 73-75).

summary: Review failed due to a blocking responsive breakpoint mismatch (implemented at md instead of required xl). Two non-blocking issues were also found: search hover border behavior mismatch and hardcoded color literals that violate token conventions.
